data.name: After AnduinOS is released, all package security updates and standard support will follow Canonical's support timeline for Ubuntu. Therefore, the corresponding LTS versions can receive up to five years of standard support.
data.value: Once AnduinOS is released, all package security updates and standard support will follow Canonical's support schedule for Ubuntu. Therefore, the corresponding LTS versions will receive up to five years of standard support.

data.name: AnduinOS 2.0 is the first release to advance the Major Version, introducing the Apkg package manager and a restructured architecture.
data.value: AnduinOS 2.0 is the first release to advance the major version, introducing the Apkg package manager and a restructured architecture.

data.name: Before AnduinOS was restructured, its Major Version was always 1.
data.value: Before AnduinOS was restructured, its Major Version was always 1.

data.name: Each Minor Version, such as 1.1, will continue to receive bug fix updates. These updates increase its Patch Version, for example, upgrading from 1.1.0 to 1.1.1. We always recommend users download the latest Patch Version within their chosen minor version.
data.value: Each Minor Version, such as 1.1, will continue to receive bug-fix updates. These updates increase its Patch Version, for example, upgrading from 1.1.0 to 1.1.1. We always recommend users download the latest Patch Version within their chosen minor version.

data.name: Each time AnduinOS updates its corresponding Ubuntu code name, the Minor Version is incremented by 1. For example, Noble is 1.1, and Oracular is 1.2. In the future, AnduinOS will continue to align with new Ubuntu code names as they are introduced.
data.value: Each time AnduinOS updates its corresponding Ubuntu code name, the Minor Version is incremented by 1. For example, Noble is 1.1, and Oracular is 1.2. In the future, AnduinOS will continue to align with new Ubuntu code names as they are introduced.

data.name: Expand a version to see details and download torrent files. Only torrents and checksums are available for historical versions.
data.value: Expand a version to view details and download torrent files. Only torrents and checksums are available for historical versions.

data.name: It no longer receives security updates or bug fixes. We strongly recommend upgrading to a supported version.
data.value: It no longer receives security updates or bug fixes. We strongly recommend upgrading to a supported version.

data.name: Like Ubuntu, AnduinOS offers both LTS versions and short-term support versions. We recommend regular users download the latest LTS version, which is currently 2.0.
data.value: Like Ubuntu, AnduinOS offers both LTS versions and short-term support versions. We recommend regular users download the latest LTS version, which is currently 2.0.

data.name: Standard support duration is determined by Canonical's support timeline for the corresponding Ubuntu release.
data.value: Standard support duration is determined by Canonical’s support timeline for the corresponding Ubuntu release.

data.name: These are legacy versions of AnduinOS. We strongly recommend downloading the latest version instead.
data.value: These are legacy versions of AnduinOS. We strongly recommend downloading the latest version instead.

data.name: This version has reached End of Life.
data.value: This version has reached End of Life.
